Hey, I noticed something weird with my smart thermostat too. Every time I get really frustrated or stressed out, the temp in my house shifts like 3 degrees instantly. Even when I don't touch the app. I think it might be the same thing happening to you. It's possible that the sensors are picking up more than just ambient air temp. They might be reacting to your physical state, like body heat. This could be a sign that your thermostat is more sensitive than we thought. Maybe it's not just a coincidence. Keep an eye on it and let me know if you notice anything else. It might be worth checking if the sensor placement is affecting the readings. Good luck!

You might be dealing with a smart thermostat that has temperature sensors in the air intake area, like the Nest Thermostat. These sensors can pick up your body heat, which might increase when you're stressed or frustrated. This rise in temperature can cause the thermostat to adjust the temperature in your home. While it doesn't read your emotional state directly, your body heat can definitely influence its readings. If this happens frequently, consider adjusting the sensor placement. Your intuition about the thermostat reacting to your emotional state is spot on, and it's a fascinating insight into how interconnected our bodies and our environments can be. Keep an eye on it and make adjustments as needed to maintain your comfort.
